title: Life on the Mississippi
author: Mark Twain
evidence_summary: Useful for the persona's narrative voice: Twain blends river pilot memory, comic exaggeration, practical observation, and vernacular timing to make explanation feel like lived experience rather than formal criticism.
notes: Public-domain source suitable for open-source citation. Helps anchor the persona's storyteller cadence, American vernacular, and habit of moving from anecdote to judgment.
